  "decision": "fail",

  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Conductors undersized for combined load and circuit length resulting in excessive voltage drop",
    "Selection based solely on ampacity per Table 310.16 without accounting for voltage drop over circuit length",
    "Non-compliant with NEC 310 and practical performance requirements for commercial wiring"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop installation of undersized conductors immediately",
    "Recalculate conductor sizing incorporating both ampacity from Table 310.16 and voltage drop for actual circuit length",
    "Pull correctly sized conductors to maintain voltage drop within acceptable limits (typically 3% for branch circuits, 5% total feeder plus branch)",
    "Document change as a submittal revision if conductor size differs from approved drawings"
  ],
  "rationale": "Table 310.16 provides minimum ampacity ratings but does not account for voltage drop over distance. In commercial work, conductors must satisfy both ampacity and voltage drop constraints. Undersized conductors that cause excessive voltage drop are non-compliant and create a defective installation that can cause equipment malfunction, overheating at connections, and reduced equipment life. Since work is in-progress, corrective action now avoids costly rework later.",
  "references": ["NEC 310", "Table 310.16"]
}
